scope.summary:
Goal: define and enforce a unified undo scope policy across all editor panes (code, rich-text, graph, canvas), integrating the undo stack with the InteractionBus (MT-031). Handshake's rule: Ctrl+Z always undoes the most recent action in the focused pane; cross-pane actions (e.g. route-to-stage, embed-from-atelier) are recorded as atomic operations in the cross-pane undo stack. Expected diff: (a) a new UndoStack type in src/frontend/shared/undo_stack.rs that holds undo/redo rings per pane and a cross-pane ring; (b) each pane registers its local undo/redo with the bus; (c) the bus dispatches Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y to the focused pane's local undo first, falling back to the cross-pane ring; (d) unit tests proving undo/redo round-trips within and across panes.

The key policy decisions this MT must encode:
  POLICY-1 (local-first): Ctrl+Z in a focused pane undoes the most recent action IN THAT PANE, not the most recent action globally. This matches VS Code and Obsidian behavior.
  POLICY-2 (cross-pane ring): actions that touch multiple panes atomically (embed-from-atelier in MT-033, route-to-stage in MT-033, code-ref insertion in MT-034) are pushed to a cross-pane undo ring. A dedicated 'Undo Cross-Pane' command (Ctrl+Shift+Z) undoes the most recent cross-pane action.
  POLICY-3 (persistence scope): undo history is session-scoped only; it is NOT persisted to the backend. On app restart, undo history is empty. Document this explicitly in code comments.
  POLICY-4 (canvas undo): canvas node moves/creations that call the backend immediately (PUT /canvas/{id}/graph) are NOT in-memory undoable via this stack alone — they require a compensating backend call (re-PUT the previous graph). The cross-pane ring records the compensating call as the undo action.
  POLICY-5 (undo stack cap): local pane undo rings are capped at 200 operations; cross-pane ring is capped at 50 operations. Exceeding the cap silently drops the oldest entry.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/shared/undo_stack.rs  — defines:
      UndoAction { description: String, undo_fn: Arc<dyn Fn() -> UndoResult + Send + Sync>, redo_fn: Arc<dyn Fn() -> UndoResult + Send + Sync> }
      UndoResult { ok: bool, error: Option<String> }
      PaneUndoRing { pane_id: PaneId, ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, redo_ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, cap: usize }
      CrossPaneUndoRing { ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, redo_ring: VecDeque<UndoAction>, cap: usize }
      UnifiedUndoScope { pane_rings: HashMap<PaneId, PaneUndoRing>, cross_pane_ring: CrossPaneUndoRing }
      Methods: push_local(pane_id, action), undo_local(pane_id) -> Option<UndoResult>, redo_local(pane_id) -> Option<UndoResult>, push_cross_pane(action), undo_cross_pane() -> Option<UndoResult>, redo_cross_pane() -> Option<UndoResult>.
  - src/frontend/tests/undo_scope_tests.rs  — unit and integration tests.

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/shared/mod.rs  — add `pub mod undo_stack;`
  - src/frontend/shared/interaction_bus.rs  — add UnifiedUndoScope as a field of InteractionBus. Add methods: bus.push_undo_local(pane_id, action), bus.undo(pane_id) (dispatches to local ring first), bus.undo_cross_pane(), bus.redo(pane_id). Register 'undo' command (Ctrl+Z) and 'redo' command (Ctrl+Y) on the command bus. Register 'undo-cross-pane' command (Ctrl+Shift+Z).
  - src/frontend/panes/code_editor.rs  — replace any existing per-pane undo state with bus.push_undo_local calls. Each text edit (character insert, delete, paste) pushes a UndoAction where undo_fn restores the previous rope state.
  - src/frontend/panes/rich_text_editor.rs  — same: each block mutation (insert node, delete node, update attrs) pushes to bus.push_undo_local. The undo_fn restores the previous document tree snapshot.
  - src/frontend/panes/graph_pane.rs  — node moves and tag changes push to bus.push_undo_local.
  - src/frontend/panes/canvas_pane.rs  — canvas graph mutations push to bus.push_cross_pane (because they call the backend); undo_fn re-PUTs the previous canvas graph.
  - src/frontend/panes/stage_pane.rs  — route-to-stage pushes to bus.push_cross_pane; undo_fn reverts StageContent to previous value.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. UndoAction closures capture the minimum state needed. For code/rich-text local undo: capture a clone of the rope/doc tree snapshot BEFORE the edit. undo_fn replaces current rope/doc with snapshot; redo_fn re-applies the edit.
  2. Rope snapshot cloning (ropey::Rope) is O(1) due to ropey's arc-based persistent data structure — safe to clone per edit.
  3. Rich-text doc snapshot: clone the JSONContent tree (serde_json::Value). For large documents this is O(n); apply a debounce: group edits within the same 500ms window into a single undo entry to avoid O(n) per keystroke.
  4. Canvas undo: the undo_fn for a canvas node creation calls PUT /canvas/{canvas_id}/graph with the previous nodes list (minus the created node). This is a compensating backend call. It must be async; in the UndoAction, undo_fn should be an async fn wrapped in Arc<dyn Fn() -> BoxFuture<UndoResult>>. Alternatively, use a channel to dispatch the compensating call to the async runtime.
  5. Keybind dispatch: in bus.dispatch_keybind, match Ctrl+Z -> undo(focused_pane_id), Ctrl+Y -> redo(focused_pane_id), Ctrl+Shift+Z -> undo_cross_pane(). Each call returns Option<UndoResult>; if Some(result) and !result.ok, log the error to the Flight Recorder (MT-036).
  6. Undo menu indicator: in the code pane and rich-text pane title bar, show a small 'Undo ({n})' indicator using the count of items in the local ring. AccessKit: author_id="undo-count-{pane_id}", role=StaticText.
  7. Tests: (a) unit: push 3 actions to a PaneUndoRing, undo 3 times, assert all undo_fns called in reverse order; (b) unit: redo after undo restores state; (c) unit: pushing beyond cap=5 drops the oldest; (d) unit: cross-pane ring undo calls undo_fn of last cross-pane action; (e) integration (real PG): create rich doc, push an undo action that saves a previous version, call undo, assert the doc reverts (use saveRichDocument to a previous content_json); (f) kittest: simulate Ctrl+Z, assert bus.undo called for focused pane.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-031 (InteractionBus) must be merged; UnifiedUndoScope is embedded in InteractionBus.
  - ropey crate must already be in Cargo.toml (from MT-001 code editor).
  - Canvas undo requires async capability in UndoAction closures; ensure the app's async runtime (tokio) is accessible from within the undo_fn.

Risk if missed: without a unified undo policy, each pane implements its own ad-hoc undo (or none), users experience unexpected behavior (Ctrl+Z in one pane undoes another, or cross-pane edits are not undoable at all), and the parity proof suite cannot assert 'one unified undo scope' as required by the interconnection contract.
acceptance_criteria: POLICY-1 (local-first): Ctrl+Z in the code pane undoes the last code edit and does NOT affect the rich-text pane's document state — proven by kittest: make edit in code pane, make edit in rich-text pane, focus code pane, press Ctrl+Z, assert only code pane reverted.

POLICY-2 (cross-pane ring): after an embed-from-atelier action (MT-033) or route-to-stage action, pressing Ctrl+Shift+Z undoes the cross-pane action — proven by kittest: trigger cross-pane action, press Ctrl+Shift+Z, assert action reverted.

POLICY-3 (persistence scope): on app restart, undo history is empty — proven by unit test: create UndoStack, serialize/deserialize, assert all rings are empty (the type must NOT implement Serialize to prevent accidental persistence).

POLICY-4 (canvas undo): undoing a canvas node creation via Ctrl+Shift+Z calls the compensating PUT /canvas/{id}/graph and the node disappears from the next canvas reload — proven by integration test against real PG.
POLICY-5 (cap): pushing 201 actions to a capped-at-200 ring results in 200 entries, not 201 — proven by unit test.
Undo count indicator shows correct count in pane title bar after pushes and undos — proven by kittest: push 3 actions, assert count=3, undo once, assert count=2.
cargo test -p handshake_native_frontend undo_scope -- --nocapture passes with no failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_native_frontend undo_scope -- --nocapture  =>  all PASS
kittest: make edit in code pane, make edit in rich-text pane, Ctrl+Z in code pane, assert only code pane reverted
kittest: trigger cross-pane embed action, Ctrl+Shift+Z, assert cross-pane action reverted
integration test (real PG): canvas node creation, Ctrl+Shift+Z undo, reload canvas, assert node absent
unit test: push 201 to cap-200 ring, assert len==200
implementation_notes: ropey::Rope clones are O(1) due to arc-based persistent structure — safe to clone for every code edit undo snapshot. Rich-text JSONContent clones are O(n) — apply the 500ms batching window.

For canvas async undo: define UndoAction.undo_async_fn: Option<Arc<dyn Fn() -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output=UndoResult> + Send>> + Send + Sync>>. Local pane undos use the sync undo_fn; canvas undo uses undo_async_fn. The bus dispatches sync and async variants separately.

The React codebase has no shared undo architecture to port from (each surface used its own). This MT is a greenfield design. The VS Code undo model (per-editor undo ring, no cross-editor) and Obsidian's per-note undo model are the behavioral references.
Do NOT attempt to implement CRDT-based undo here; CRDT is handled by the CRDT backend (MT-038/MT-039). This undo stack is purely the in-memory UI-layer undo for immediate local feedback.
ports_from_react: No direct React parity source — the React version has no shared undo implementation. VS Code undo model (per-editor ring) and Obsidian per-note undo are behavioral references.
binds_backend_api: PUT /canvas/{canvas_id}/graph  =>  api.ts updateCanvasGraph  =>  used in canvas undo compensating call to restore previous graph state
POST /knowledge/documents/{doc_id}  =>  api.ts saveRichDocument  =>  used in rich-text undo compensating call to restore previous content_json (only for cross-pane undo; local undo is in-memory only)

risks: Rich-text doc snapshot per keystroke at O(n) causes lag on large documents if the 500ms batching window is not implemented or if serde_json cloning is unexpectedly slow.
Canvas async undo_fn holding a stale copy of the previous canvas graph: if the user makes further canvas edits before the undo fires, the compensating PUT may clobber the intermediate edits.
UndoAction closures capturing Arc references to pane state create retain cycles if pane state is also held by the InteractionBus; must use Weak references for back-references.
Ctrl+Z keybind conflict with text-widget default undo in egui: if egui processes Ctrl+Z for its own text fields before the bus, the bus undo never fires in text-input contexts.
Cross-pane undo ring may interleave with local pane undo if the user alternates pane actions rapidly, confusing the undo order visible to the user.
minimum_controls: Implement and unit-test the 500ms batching window for rich-text undo; assert that rapid keystrokes within 500ms produce one undo entry, not N.
Canvas async undo: record the graph snapshot at the time of the action creation, not at the time of undo invocation. Store the snapshot in the UndoAction closure.
Use Weak<Mutex<PaneState>> in UndoAction closures; upgrade to Arc only during undo invocation; skip silently if the pane was dropped.
In bus.dispatch_keybind for Ctrl+Z: call ui.input_mut(|i| i.consume_shortcut(&ctrl_z)) before delegating to undo, so egui's text-widget handler does not also fire.
Expose a visual undo history inspector (accessible via command palette: 'Show Undo History') showing the last 10 local + cross-pane entries; this makes the policy testable by humans and swarm agents.
